title: Dark-bodied subjects re-ink outlines dark
oneline: Deterministic rim-erase punch extension validated; blanket dilation risky; Gemini edit re-rolls fills
summary: The surviving dark rim is a mid-dark penumbra (raw luma ~80-140) hugging the outside of chalk strokes on lineW-flagged pages. The validated fix extends the night punch mask by pixels within 2px of chalk ink with raw luma in [55,145) and inpaints them with the standard bleed — rim gone on train-wide's face, owl's near-black eye ring and cat's dark straps untouched, control page visually unchanged. Blanket dilation r=2 erodes pupils/catchlights, and the literal 'paint them white' adds white halos. The Gemini 'whiten outlines, change nothing else' edit fixes lineW (75 to 206-224) but redraws eyes and fills (3.6-14.9% drift), so it is a regeneration lever, not a post-process.
tried: (a) paint-white at chalk-line positions (literal IDEAS.md wording)
(a) rim-delta selective punch (vs dilate-4 reference punch)
(a) FINAL: raw luma in [55,145) within 2px of chalk, lineW page gate
(c) blanket punch-mask dilation r=1 and r=2
(b) Gemini image-edit 'make every outline bright white, change nothing else' (5 calls: 3 train-wide, 2 cat-wide)
rim-delta band metric + hotspot ranking to locate/quantify the rim (doubles as idea #7 machinery)
worked: Final rule (a): mask += pixels within 2px of chalk ink with raw luma in [55,145), inpainted by the standard bleed, gated to pages with lineW < 150 — removes train-wide's mouth/nose/face rim (band rim share 0.20% -> 0.08%), composite change confined to 0.117% of pixels
Near-black floor (55) protects deliberate ink: owl-tall's bold eye ring survives even ungated
Legit dark fills adjacent to lines (cat bale straps, night sky) re-bleed their own color — visually unchanged
Control page (shapes/circle-wide, lineW 251) is a natural no-op
(b) reliably whitens outlines: lineW 75 -> 206/213/224 on train-wide, beating ~27 fresh-generation attempts
failed: Paint-white (literal idea (a)): white halos around mouth/chin — visibly worse
Blanket dilate r=2 (idea (c)): erodes the train's pupil edge and catchlight, beige contamination in the sclera; r=1 is safe but leaves half the rim
a-dark without the near-black floor: bleeds the owl's ~6px black eye ring away to white — flagship-page regression
Thin-structure opening: cannot separate a ~5px re-ink shadow from a ~6px legit ring (protected both, fixed nothing)
(b) 'change nothing else' is never honored: best take still redrew the eye, recolored the tongue, added glow; cat-wide take dropped the bale's red straps (3.6-14.9% of fill pixels drift)
limitations: The speckle ring inside the train's eye is the raw's own soft pupil edge >2px from any chalk ink — unreachable by punch-mask fixes (ideas #15/#7 territory)

Bounds 55/145/r=2 calibrated on 7 pages; a page with deliberate mid-dark art 1-2px wide beside a chalk line would lose it — the lineW page gate (only train-wide qualifies today) keeps exposure near-zero
(b) tested at t=0.2 only, 5 takes total, one model (gemini-2.5-flash-image)

images.caption: Where the rim actually is on train-wide (red = pixels >25 luma darker than the local fill, within 3px of chalk ink): the face outline, mouth, nose — not the dark boiler
cat-wide rim flags are mostly false positives on the hay bale's legit dark straps — why naive delta-selection can't be trusted
Pixels changed by the un-floored a-dark rule on owl-tall — concentrated exactly on the eye rings it destroys
(b) drift evidence: the Gemini edit reshaped and moved the pupil
train-wide full composite under the final rule
owl eye ring, committed state (the thing the fix must not break)

code.title: RECOMMENDED: rim-erase punch extension (addRimOverhang + standalone demo)
Rim measurement: dilate-4 reference punch, band delta stats, hotspot ranking (reusable for idea #7)
Shared machinery: chalk mask, ring bands, punch/bleed, night composite, crops
Experiment harness for variants (c) dilate, (a) selective, (a) paint-white
Experiment: raw-dark mask extension (no floor) — the owl-regression variant
(b) Gemini whiten-outlines image-edit with offline scoring
